All right, your back time for your bonus lesson for creating customers for life. It's a great one. So we've already been through referrals. How do you ask for them? How do you build a steady stream of them using the straight line system? Right? And they say to you, well, whoa, whoa, whoa, I can't do that. I don't even know you have you haven't proved yourself to me yet. How do I tell my friends when I haven't seen results? Right? Fair question. Maybe maybe not. Here's the deal. It's simple to overcome. So let's go through that right now with Sarah interviewing me and I'll see you on the other side and fill in a few more blanks. I'll see you soon. All right. So let's say you closed someone, you're selling insurance, you closed that policy and you asked that person that you closed, hey, do you know anybody else that might be in need of this product? And the person responds and says, I can't give you any referrals until I see you perform. How do you respond to that? Does that set up the stage for confrontation? Good question. So yeah, right. So obviously my system for getting referrals is don't wait. The best time to ask for referrals right after you close someone, right? Good question. Does that happen? From time to time it does happen, but that's not a negative thing. Let's say you go through a sale and you do a great job for someone and you fill in the there, you close the deal and then after it's done as you're writing up the paperwork, oh, by the way, do you know anybody else who I could help with the same situation who's suffering in the same way that you were like that? Right? And they say, well, yeah, I know, but I mean, until I see what you do. I don't feel comfortable, right? I've been a bit, oh my, oh, I'm so sorry. I would say, oh, no problem at all. All I ask is this, okay? In a few weeks where you really see how awesome this is, I want you to give your referrals. That sounds good. It's so easy to get around that and it comes from a place of confidence. I mean, let me just circle back for a second. In other words, the whole idea that you have to wait to ask someone for referrals, some arbitrary time. Flawed proposition. Psychologically, people, when they make a decision and say, yes, I'll do it. What they've done in their own mind, they've crossed over their action threshold. They become certain enough collectively with those three 10s, they say, yep, I'm ready to go forward, right? But of course, in their own mind, it's not a conviction. It's some seeds of doubt probably still, right? This is really, really a situation where they're really certain, right? So what do people want to do? They want to almost confirm their own decision by having other people become part of it. They want to create consensus. So almost by having other people jump on what do, it makes them feel better about their own decision. Like, well, why did I say yes? If they're still skeptical of that guy, they should have said yes. In other words, it's almost like this psychological principle when someone wants everyone to jump on the same bandwagon they're on, which is why it's so common. I've seen this happen so many, I'm telling you so many times, Sarah, where I am selling someone, whether it's in the meat industry, whether it's in mortgages, whether it's in insurance or stops or anything else, stereosurface will do everything. The second someone's done with their purchase, and if you'll really develop that tight rapport like you do with the straight line and you ask that question, they're like, oh my God, let me take that, and they'll take out their phone that will start working for you because they want to feel good about their decision. It almost confirms in their own mind they made the right decision. So there's absolutely, positively nothing wrong with asking for referrals respectfully using the system, the language patterns I created and you already learned that here, right guys? You've already been through that. There's the bonus lesson here. There is nothing wrong with doing this. In fact, it promotes a positive long term relationship because they now they feel like they've almost moved past now that whether it's good or not. They've like locked down their decision now. They're not going to cancel. They got their friends on board. That's a very, very powerful thing. And I think all in all, just to close this out with referrals, to me, referrals are by far the best business of all. And when you have someone who's an active refer, those relationships become the closest ones of all. When someone starts referring business to you, it makes them a better customer. They'll buy more things from you. They're fully invested in your process. So don't be shy about this. Don't be bashful when you're done. If it's appropriate, you ask for referrals. There's no arbitrary reason why you have to wait. Definitely the best time right after you close the deal or if not that shortly thereafter. All right, pretty cool stuff, right? One final point before we go and I'll finish yet, but I want to finish this module. I've got a final note for you before we wrap this whole thing up. It's been an amazing journey together, right? Just one point on referrals. Remember, after you get hit with that objection, so to speak of, how do I give you referrals without already seeing results, so to speak, right? Well, just remember, after you employ the strategy that I recommended, you can also ask again a few minutes later because what you'll find by the way is that even after you answer that objection, you could also loop back and ask for referrals again if the opportunity presents itself. Just remember that you always want to balance that off with ending the close on a high point. So full out of rapport, seem too pushy, but you will find though that even though someone balks at the first chance, you have a few more words with that person. They start to feel bad about not giving you a referral and the opportunity arises again to sliding that question that perhaps maybe there's some urgency because the price is about to go up or there's some scarcity involved.
